title: Fixing React Memoized Component Re-renders Due to Changing Object References

statement.markdown:
Write a React memoized component that displays the number of clicks. When the user clicks a button, the number should increment. The component should only re-render when the number of clicks changes.

Your task is to fix the buggy memoized component that is re-rendering unnecessarily due to changing object references.
statement.inputFormat: Input is an object with a single property 'clicks' initialized to 0.
statement.outputFormat: Output is the number of clicks as a string.
statement.notes: Use the `useCallback` and `useMemo` hooks to memoize the component.

examples.explanation: After 5 clicks, the number of clicks is 5
After 10 clicks, the number of clicks is 10

hints.text: Use `useCallback` to memoize the button click handler

editorial.title: Editorial Title
editorial.intuition: The component should re-render only when the number of clicks changes.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use `useCallback` and `useMemo` to memoize the component and its dependencies.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The component re-renders only when the number of clicks changes.
